Title: Kaempferol 3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-rhamnoside is an endogenous flavonol inhibitor of polar auxin transport in Arabidopsis shoots

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavonols and anthocyanidins; specifically kaempferol/quercetin aglycone 3-O acceptors
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-O-glucoside flavonol derivatives including k2, k3, q2 and q3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 3-O position of flavonol substrates, producing flavonol 3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous inhibitor of basipetal polar auxin transport, affecting shoot stature and gravitropism
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard growth conditions; PAT assay with [3H]-IAA and NPA; natural-like high-light sun simulator conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of UGT78D2 caused dwarf shoot stature, reduced stem parenchyma cell length, reduced apical dominance, and delayed gravitropism. 2. UGT78D2 complementation restored both wild-type growth and flavonol glycoside pattern. 3. ugt78d2 strongly reduced 3-O-glucoside flavonol derivatives while elevating kaempferol 3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-rhamnoside (k1), which negatively correlated with basipetal auxin transport. 4. The growth defect was flavonol-dependent because tt4 or fls1 suppressed the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of UGT78D2 promotes accumulation of kaempferol 3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-rhamnoside and reduces basipetal auxin transport, leading to shorter shoots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant analysis; genomic complementation; double-mutant epistasis; HPLC flavonol profiling; [3H]-IAA transport assay; IAA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT78D2 itself does not make the target metabolite; rather, its loss redirects flux away from 3-O-glucosides toward 3-O-rhamnosylated flavonols, enabling k1 accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function causes dwarfism, altered branching/apical dominance, and delayed gravitropism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating UGT78D2 can tune flavonol glycoside composition and shoot architecture, but reduced growth is a major tradeoff.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-O-rhamnoside flavonol derivatives including k1 precursor and q1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to the 3-O position of flavonol substrates, enabling formation of kaempferol 3-O-rhamnosylated products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous inhibitor of basipetal polar auxin transport in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively abundant at shoot apex and almost absent from roots (from supporting/discussion)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT78D1 catalyzes 3-O-rhamnosylation required for k1 formation. 2. The ugt78d1 ugt78d2 double mutant lacked k1 and reverted the ugt78d2 dwarf phenotype to wild type. 3. Its expression pattern is consistent with low root k1 and shoot-specific action of k1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for production of the active kaempferol bis-rhamnoside that represses shoot polar auxin transport
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis with ugt78d2; HPLC flavonol profiling; phenotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of the identified active metabolite k1; discussed as abundant in shoots versus roots, supporting organ-specific function.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces k1 and suppresses ugt78d2-associated dwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to lower inhibitory k1 accumulation and alleviate growth repression in shoots.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-O-rhamnosylated kaempferol derivative leading to k1 backbone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to the 7-O position of the kaempferol glycoside backbone to produce k1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous inhibitor of basipetal polar auxin transport in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT89C1 catalyzes the 7-O-rhamnosylation needed for k1 formation. 2. In ugt78d2 ugt89c1 double mutants, k1 was absent and the dwarf ugt78d2 phenotype reverted to wild type. 3. This genetic evidence strongly supports k1 as the causal metabolite.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for accumulation of kaempferol 3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-rhamnoside and thus for the auxin transport-inhibitory metabolic state
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis with ugt78d2; HPLC flavonol profiling; phenotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unknown flavonol derivatives accumulated in ugt78d2 ugt89c1, but loss of k1 specifically abolished the dwarf phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reduce inhibitory k1 without eliminating total flavonoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3'-hydroxylation converting kaempferol-type intermediates toward quercetin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous inhibitor of basipetal polar auxin transport in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Blocking TT7 removed quercetin derivatives and increased k1 accumulation. 2. tt7 ugt78d2 plants were more severely dwarfed and had even lower basipetal auxin transport than ugt78d2. 3. These data showed quercetin derivatives were not required and strengthened the causal role of kaempferol bis-rhamnoside k1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TT7 increases k1 accumulation and enhances repression of basipetal auxin transport and shoot growth defects in the ugt78d2 background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis with ugt78d2; HPLC flavonol profiling; [3H]-IAA transport assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used here mainly as a pathway-dissection gene to distinguish kaempferol versus quercetin contribution.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced dwarfism when combined with ugt78d2
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Caution: redirecting flux away from quercetin toward kaempferol can exacerbate growth repression via k1 accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes entry into flavonoid biosynthesis by forming chalcone backbone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous inhibitor of basipetal polar auxin transport in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Blocking TT4 eliminated flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. The tt4 ugt78d2 double mutant rescued the ugt78d2 dwarf phenotype. 3. This established that the growth defect was flavonoid dependent and not due simply to loss of UGT78D2 products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TT4 suppresses k1-associated shoot growth defects by abolishing flavonoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant epistasis; phenotype analysis; HPLC profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as upstream pathway block to show flavonoid dependence of the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Removal of all flavonoids may affect multiple traits beyond the focal phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mechanistic control but too pleiotropic for targeted engineering of growth via k1 alone.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Nö-derived fls1 crossed into Col-0 ugt78d2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols, providing flavonol aglycone backbones for glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endogenous inhibitor of basipetal polar auxin transport in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fls1 specifically blocks biosynthesis of flavonol moieties. 2. Introgression of fls1 into ugt78d2 restored wild-type-like growth. 3. This further demonstrated that the ugt78d2 phenotype depended on flavonols rather than anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of FLS1 suppresses the ugt78d2 growth phenotype by preventing flavonol production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background accession differs from Col-0; used as an independent genetic test for flavonol dependence.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Blocking flavonol synthesis broadly may alter UV/stress-related functions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mechanistically informative but broad flavonol loss may be undesirable in applications.
